controlNotes: The Soundcraft Ui12 is a browser-controlled digital mixer — connect your device to the Ui12's Wi-Fi network (or Ethernet), then open a Chrome browser and navigate to the mixer's IP address (default: 192.168.1.1 / ui12.local). Each channel has a fully parametric 4-band EQ plus a sweepable HPF. Tap a channel strip to open its full processing view. Phantom power (+48V) and input gain are in the Preamp tab. NOTE: The Ui12's USB port is a 2-channel media player only (MP3/WAV/AIFF playback) — it does NOT function as a multi-channel audio interface to a DAW. To analyze individual channels in SoundPilot, use the mixer's Solo/PFL output routed to your audio interface input and solo each channel in turn.

instructionTemplates.selectChannel: In the Soundcraft Ui app, tap the CH {channel} strip at the top to select it and open its channel view
instructionTemplates.openEQ: In the channel view, tap the EQ tab to open the 4-band parametric EQ
instructionTemplates.enableHPF: In the EQ tab, tap the HPF button to enable the high-pass filter, then drag the HPF frequency knob to {frequency}Hz
instructionTemplates.setBandFrequency: Tap the {band} band, then drag the Frequency knob (or double-tap to type) to {frequency}Hz
instructionTemplates.setBandGain: Drag the {band} Gain knob {direction} to set approximately {amount}dB. The response curve updates in real time.
instructionTemplates.saveSettings: Tap the menu icon (top-right) and choose Save Show to save your current settings to a show file
